---
title: "Item Text"
---

## Description of the IRW item text data

The below scatterplot shows the mean number of words (when `item_text` is split by ' ') and characters in the items for the IRW tables with associated item text (along with the name of the relevant psychometric instrument).



```{r}
#| label: load-data
#| include: false

library(redivis)

user <- redivis$user("bdomingu")
dataset <- user$dataset("irw_meta:bdxt")
table <- dataset$table("itemtext_metadata:drat")

# Load table as tidyverse tibble
data <- table$to_tibble()
data <- as.data.frame(data)
data$mean_word <- as.numeric(data$mean_word)
data$mean_character <- as.numeric(data$mean_character)
data$instrument <- gsub("\n","",data$instrument)

print(str(data))  # Let this show

# Pass data to Observable
ojs_define(plot_data = data)
```

```{ojs}
//| label: scatterplot

// Import Observable Plot
Plot = require("@observablehq/plot")

// Transpose the data from column format to row format
plotData = plot_data.table.map((d, i) => ({
  table: plot_data.table[i],
  instrument: plot_data.instrument[i],
  mean_word: plot_data.mean_word[i],
  mean_character: plot_data.mean_character[i]
}))

// Create scatterplot with enhanced tooltips
Plot.plot({
  marks: [
    Plot.dot(plotData, {
      x: "mean_word",
      y: "mean_character",
      title: d => `Table: ${d.table}\nInstrument: ${d.instrument}\nMean Word: ${d.mean_word.toFixed(0)}\nMean Characters: ${d.mean_character.toFixed(0)}`,
      tip: true
    })
  ],
  x: {
    label: "Mean Word"
  },
  y: {
    label: "Mean Characters"
  }
})
```
